name: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B
tagline: Google's first open-weight text-diffusion model, generating text up to 4x faster.
metaDescription: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B is Google's open-weight text-diffusion MoE model on a Gemma 4 backbone, generating text up to 4x faster with a 256K context window.
architecture: Mixture-of-Experts (8 active of 128 experts + 1 shared) on a Gemma 4 backbone, using discrete diffusion with block-autoregressive sampling
overview: DiffusionGemma is Google DeepMind's first open-weight text-diffusion model, released June 10, 2026. Instead of generating text token-by-token like a standard autoregressive LLM, it starts from a canvas of placeholder tokens and iteratively denoises blocks of text in parallel, using Uniform State Diffusion and Block-Autoregressive Diffusion for variable-length generation.

Built on a Gemma 4 Mixture-of-Experts backbone, the 26B-A4B variant has 25.2B total parameters but activates only 3.8B per token (8 of 128 experts plus 1 shared expert). The parallel diffusion approach delivers up to 4x faster text generation on GPUs — Google reports 1000+ tokens/sec on a single NVIDIA H100 and 700+ tokens/sec on an RTX 5090. Google notes overall output quality is below standard Gemma 4 and recommends Gemma 4 where maximum quality matters.

The model accepts text and image (and video) input and produces text output, with a context window of up to 256K tokens and a January 2025 knowledge cutoff. Weights are released under Apache 2.0 on Hugging Face, Kaggle, Vertex AI Model Garden, and the NVIDIA NIM catalog.
strengths: First open-weight Google text-diffusion model, released under Apache 2.0
Up to 4x faster generation via parallel block diffusion (1000+ tok/s on an H100)
Sparse MoE design activates only 3.8B of 25.2B parameters, easing deployment
Multimodal input (text and image) on a Gemma 4 backbone
useCases: Latency-sensitive and real-time interactive text generation
Edge and local deployment where throughput is the binding constraint
Self-hosted open-weight applications needing fast inference

faq.a: DiffusionGemma is Google DeepMind's first open-weight text-diffusion model, released June 10, 2026. Instead of generating text one token at a time, it denoises blocks of tokens in parallel from a placeholder canvas. The 26B-A4B variant is a Mixture-of-Experts model on a Gemma 4 backbone, released under the Apache 2.0 license.

Google reports DiffusionGemma generates text up to 4x faster than comparable autoregressive models on GPUs, thanks to its parallel block-diffusion approach. Published figures include over 1000 tokens per second on a single NVIDIA H100 and over 700 tokens per second on an NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090.

faq.q: How many parameters does DiffusionGemma have?
faq.a: The 26B-A4B variant has 25.2B total parameters but activates only 3.8B per token, using a Mixture-of-Experts design with 8 active experts out of 128 plus 1 shared expert. This sparse activation keeps inference efficient and lets the model run on modest hardware when quantized.

Yes. DiffusionGemma's weights are released under the Apache 2.0 license and are available to download from Hugging Face, Kaggle, Google Cloud's Vertex AI Model Garden, and the NVIDIA NIM catalog. Community quantized builds, including an NVFP4 version from NVIDIA, are also available.

faq.q: How does DiffusionGemma compare to Gemma 4 in quality?
faq.a: Google states that DiffusionGemma's overall output quality is lower than standard Gemma 4, and recommends Gemma 4 for applications that need maximum quality. DiffusionGemma is aimed instead at latency-sensitive and real-time use cases where its up-to-4x faster generation speed is the main advantage.
